about_text1: Squadron is a member of the ASE Group with over a decade of experience in the unmanned aircraft industry. We focus on providing safe and comprehensive unmanned aviation products and services, safety consulting, and software.
about_text2: We owe our market position to our qualified staff, who combine their experience with innovative solutions. A team of certified pilots, instructors, and civilian-military experts guarantees the highest standards of service and training.
about_text3: Because we are the only company in Poland with the ISO - 28007 certificate, and our management has experience in implementing maritime security operations as part of NATO operations.

iryda_plus_text1: IRYDA Plus is a Polish-developed counter-drone system project (C-UAS – Counter-Unmanned Aerial Systems), implemented in a consortium model with the participation of MBF Group S.A., Squadron Sp. z o.o. and the Polish Industrial Lobby. The aim of the project is to create a specialized UAV platform designed for the detection, identification, and kinetic neutralization of unmanned aerial vehicles while maintaining a rational cost-effectiveness ratio.
iryda_plus_text2: IRYDA Plus is one of the key elements of MBF Group S.A.’s long-term development strategy in the area of modern unmanned technologies and security systems. The project also has a significant investment dimension, aligning with the growing interest of capital markets in defense and dual-use technologies. The development of competencies in this area is intended to build the company’s long-term value and strengthen its position in the advanced technology segment.
iryda_plus_text3: In recent years, unmanned aerial vehicles have become a common tool in both civil and military applications. At the same time, drones have begun to be used as relatively low-cost means of reconnaissance, disruption, and attack, which has significantly changed the nature of contemporary asymmetric threats. Conventional air defense systems, designed with other classes of targets in mind, are increasingly proving to be cost-inefficient and operationally inadequate in the fight against small, low-flying UAVs.
iryda_plus_text4: IRYDA Plus was created as a response to this gap – as a specialized kinetic UAV interceptor capable of long-endurance air patrol and active threat interception at a much lower unit cost than conventional effectors.
iryda_plus_text5: IRYDA Plus is an unmanned aerial platform designed from the ground up to serve as an active counter-drone system. Its primary task is the detection, identification, and physical neutralization of unmanned aerial vehicles, including reconnaissance drones, strike drones, and loitering munitions.
iryda_plus_text6: The system is being developed as a complementary solution to existing defense layers, capable of operating both autonomously and in integration with other detection and command systems. Particular emphasis has been placed on operational flexibility and the ability to adapt to changing threat scenarios.
iryda_plus_text7: The foundation of the IRYDA Plus project is a modular UAV platform architecture, enabling the configuration to be tailored to specific user needs and operational conditions. The system’s open structure allows for the integration of various sensors, observation systems, and effectors, as well as further expansion as technology evolves.
iryda_plus_text8: A key role in the project is played by situational awareness systems, including optoelectronic sensors, target-tracking algorithms, and decision-support elements. IRYDA Plus is being designed with a high level of autonomy in mind while maintaining the possibility of operator supervision and control.
iryda_plus_text9: According to the current design assumptions, IRYDA Plus is being developed as an unmanned interceptor aircraft with parameters enabling the interception of targets in the mini/small UAV class and selected categories of loitering munitions. The assumed parameters include:
iryda_plus_text15: The platform is expected to use a 360° rotating turret with a lightweight 7.62 mm kinetic effector and integration with EO/IR sensors and external systems (e.g. radars, command systems). The TAS (Target–Aim–Shot) system, supported by AI algorithms, enables automatic target tracking, aiming, and real-time fire correction (edge computing).
iryda_plus_text16: The indicated parameters are design assumptions and may be subject to change during development work and testing.
iryda_plus_text17: One of the most important assumptions of IRYDA Plus is to solve the problem of the unfavorable cost-effectiveness ratio characteristic of conventional air defense systems when confronting low-cost drones. The use of kinetic target interception allows for a significant reduction in the cost of neutralizing a single threat while also enabling repeated use of the platform (in accordance with the system architecture and test results at subsequent stages).
iryda_plus_text18: This approach responds to current operational challenges, where the key factor is the ability to provide long-term area protection while maintaining rational operating costs.
iryda_plus_text19: IRYDA Plus is designed as a system with a wide range of applications. It may be used to protect critical infrastructure, industrial facilities, logistics hubs, airports, and energy installations. At the same time, the platform may serve as an active interceptor in the protection of borders and areas of particular strategic importance.
iryda_plus_text20: Due to its dual-use nature, this solution may find application both in the defense sector and in the area of public security, including preventive and patrol operations.
iryda_plus_text21: IRYDA Plus is currently in an advanced stage of design and engineering work, including the refinement of the system architecture, integration of key components, and preparation of technology demonstrators. The project is being implemented in stages, with the assumption of gradual progression from conceptual solutions to prototypes and operational testing.
iryda_plus_text22: This approach allows for the systematic reduction of technological risks and the flexible adjustment of the development direction to changing market and regulatory conditions.
iryda_plus_text23: The IRYDA Plus project is implemented in a consortium model, combining engineering, industrial, and expert competencies. The cooperation between the partners aims to create a sustainable technological base capable not only of delivering a single project, but also of further developing unmanned and counter-drone systems in the future.
iryda_plus_text24: This model remains open to expanding cooperation with additional technological, scientific, and industrial partners in the areas of component integration, research, testing, production, and preparation for commercialization stages.
iryda_plus_text25: As the project develops, MBF Group S.A. is consistently strengthening the competence, industrial, and integration base of IRYDA Plus. In recent months, numerous agreements and arrangements have been signed and announced (published in ESPI/EBI reports and company communications), organizing the project structurally and expanding its implementation potential.
iryda_plus_text26: Consortium model – project implementation within the cooperation of MBF Group S.A., Squadron Sp. z o.o., and the Polish Industrial Lobby (PLP), including technology development, work preparation, and staged implementation.
iryda_plus_text27: Technological and industrial partnerships – agreements and arrangements in the areas of systems integration, components, and preparation for commercialization and scaling (including integration, testing, and operational areas).
iryda_plus_text28: Expert cooperation – acquisition of scientific and aviation competencies (advisory support in areas critical to system development and research).
iryda_plus_text29: System integration and mobility – agreements related to integration with operational platforms and carriers (for selected field and infrastructure applications).
iryda_plus_text30: ROFR – reservation and right of first refusal agreement – conclusion of a reservation agreement and right of first refusal concerning the IRYDA Plus system, as an element of expanding strategic cooperation and confirming market interest (detailed terms in accordance with the Company’s communications).
iryda_plus_text31: A more complete summary of selected information on agreements and partnerships is available in materials published on the MBF Group S.A. website and in ESPI/EBI reports.
iryda_plus_text32: IRYDA Plus is designed to operate in various operational and regulatory environments. From the outset, its export potential and the possibility of adapting the system to the requirements of foreign users have been taken into account, particularly in markets where demand for cost-effective C-UAS systems is growing dynamically.
iryda_plus_text33: The project fits into the broader trend of developing modern security technologies, in which unmanned solutions and autonomous systems play a key role.
iryda_plus_text34: IRYDA Plus is one of the projects strengthening the position of MBF Group S.A. as a technology company developing its own competencies in the UAV and C-UAS area. As a company listed on the stock market, MBF Group S.A. implements a strategy based on business diversification and value creation through the development of projects with high growth potential.
iryda_plus_text35: Engagement in projects such as IRYDA Plus allows the company to participate in the dynamically growing security technology market segment while creating the foundations for future commercialization, international cooperation, and value growth for shareholders.
iryda_plus_text36: If you would like to establish cooperation in the areas of technology, integration, production, or distribution, please send a short inquiry containing: company profile, area of competence, scope of potential cooperation, preferred model (technological / production / commercial), and target market. For discussions requiring the disclosure of non-public information, standard confidentiality instruments (NDA / MNDA) are used.
iryda_plus_title2: IRYDA Plus: unmanned fighter – kinetic C-UAS interceptor
iryda_plus_title11: Key agreements, partnerships, and formalization of cooperation
iryda_plus_title13: IRYDA Plus and the long-term value of MBF Group S.A.
mallard_text1: The MALLARD project opens a new chapter in the development of Polish air transport technology. It is also a unique opportunity to join MBF Group S.A. in a venture with strong commercial and export potential. We invite companies, industry partners, as well as institutional and private investors to establish cooperation. It is possible to participate in the project technologically, operationally, and financially – in a way that may generate real profits in the future through the commercialization of the MALLARD system on European and global markets.
mallard_text2: MALLARD is a strategic project of the consortium of MBF Group S.A. (leader), Squadron Sp. z o.o. (ASE Group), and the Polish Industrial Lobby, whose ambition is to create a complete unmanned cargo transport ecosystem in Poland. The project includes two complementary platforms: MALLARD-S – a light last-mile UAV, and MALLARD-L – a heavy drone designed for long-range cargo transport with a payload of up to 1 tonne. Both versions are being developed as dual-use technologies, compliant with European U-Space requirements, and capable of operating in civil, industrial, and defense environments.
mallard_text3: The autonomous air transport market is developing at a pace never seen before in the aviation sector. It is estimated that the global cargo drone market will exceed USD 16–20 billion by 2030, while most analyses indicate annual growth rates of 20–35%. The largest segments include medical logistics, urgent B2B deliveries, spare parts transport, energy, offshore, mining, and the defense sector.
mallard_text4: The COVID-19 pandemic significantly accelerated the adoption of drones in medical transport, while the geopolitical events of recent years have shown that traditional supply chains are vulnerable to disruption, overload, and lack of redundancy. This increases demand for platforms capable of operating autonomously, rapidly, and independently of ground infrastructure constraints. MALLARD fits into this trend by offering a system designed from the outset for long-range operations and payload capacities unavailable in most current market solutions.
mallard_text5: Drone transport is transitioning from the demonstrator stage to real commercial deployment. In many countries – including the USA, France, Australia, and Japan – cargo drones are already performing regular flights, delivering medicines, documents, medical samples, rescue equipment, and courier shipments. U-Space is becoming the standard for traffic management in Europe, enabling BVLOS missions at scale.
mallard_text6: Modern cargo UAVs are beginning to replace helicopters in low- and medium-payload missions. Compared to helicopters, drones are many times cheaper to operate, more environmentally friendly, quieter, and easier to maintain. This makes their large-scale deployment not only possible, but economically justified. MALLARD has the potential to become one of the key solutions in the new class of heavy drones in Europe.
mallard_text7: The MALLARD project includes two platforms with a common architecture but different purposes. Together, they create a coherent transport system capable of supporting both urban logistics and long-range cargo missions requiring extended range and high payload capacity.
mallard_text10: MALLARD-S is a lightweight and cost-efficient platform designed for last-mile tasks and specialized transport. The system enables fast deliveries in the medical, logistics, and industrial sectors, providing autonomous route handling – from takeoff, through cruise, to landing and cargo module exchange.
mallard_text11: The S version has been designed for critical deliveries such as blood, medicines, organs, documents, spare parts, and rescue equipment. Thanks to its extended range, the platform can perform interregional missions and support logistics wherever time and independence from ground infrastructure are of key importance.
mallard_text12: Its design allows operation in urban, mountainous, and hard-to-reach areas. MALLARD-S makes it possible to build rapid air transport networks for industry, hospitals, airports, refineries, mines, wind farms, logistics centers, and rescue units.
mallard_text15: MALLARD-L is a breakthrough design being developed as a platform with a payload of up to 1 tonne and intercontinental range in its transit configuration. The system is intended to take over some of the tasks currently performed by transport helicopters and military trucks, while providing radically lower mission costs and greater operational flexibility.
mallard_text16: The MALLARD-L concept assumes the possibility of vertical takeoff or short takeoff run, allowing the UAV to operate from improvised airstrips, field runways, offshore platforms, or ship decks. The modular cargo cabin will enable the transport of industrial pallets, medical containers, machine parts, fuel, food, and field equipment. Redundant control systems and an optional hybrid propulsion system will ensure high resistance to disruption and increased autonomy.
mallard_text17: The MALLARD-S and MALLARD-L systems open up new opportunities in the civil sector by delivering solutions capable of carrying out missions with high operational and logistical value. Thanks to its mobility and range, MALLARD-S is ideally suited for medical transport — delivering blood, medicines, organs, biological materials, and time-critical equipment. In industrial areas, it enables the rapid delivery of spare parts, tools, and documentation, reducing production downtime and increasing plant efficiency. The system can also be used for monitoring energy and transmission infrastructure, transporting components to wind farms and offshore installations, as well as in mining operations, where traditional transport is often slow or risky.
mallard_text18: In turn, MALLARD-L, with a payload of up to 1,000 kg and a range of up to 3,000 km, represents an alternative to helicopter and truck transport in missions requiring high flexibility and resilience to terrain conditions. It is ideally suited for deliveries of fuel, components, and equipment for refineries, LNG terminals, offshore platforms, or remote industrial sites. In large e-commerce projects, both systems enable the rapid movement of goods between distribution centers, as well as the transport of high-value or oversized shipments not handled by standard commercial drones. Thanks to the ability to operate beyond the reach of road infrastructure, MALLARD helps ensure supply continuity even in crisis situations, natural disasters, or supply chain disruptions.
mallard_text19: In the defense sector, the MALLARD-S and MALLARD-L systems introduce an entirely new quality in autonomous battlefield logistics. The lighter MALLARD-S platform enables rapid deliveries of ammunition, spare parts, electronic components, medical supplies, food, and equipment elements without the need to involve crews and vehicles exposed to shelling or landmines. Thanks to its ability to operate in threat zones without requiring prepared infrastructure, the drone can reach places where traditional transport is too risky or impossible. In MEDEVAC missions, the MALLARD-S UAV can carry medical modules enabling the evacuation of medical supplies, medicines, or surgical equipment in situations where reaction time determines survival.
mallard_text20: The heavier MALLARD-L platform, on the other hand, opens up capabilities previously reserved exclusively for transport helicopters, but at significantly lower operating costs and lower risk. With a payload of up to 1,000 kg and intercontinental range, MALLARD-L can supply field bases, transport weapons, fuel, mortar rounds, ammunition crates, artillery system components, and mobile technical equipment. The system also ensures logistical continuity for NATO allied forces in areas with limited infrastructure and can support special operations requiring discreet, autonomous transport. Thanks to its resistance to interference and redundant control systems, MALLARD becomes an important element of the “resilient logistics” strategy, strengthening the military’s ability to operate in conditions of disruption, hybrid conflict, and a dynamically changing operational environment.
mallard_text21: Rising fuel costs, driver shortages, road congestion, regulatory changes, the need for supply chain redundancy, and the low cost per mission make cargo drones a logical successor to helicopters in light and medium cargo transport. Autonomous UAV operations reduce operating costs by as much as 60–80% compared to small helicopters, making investment in systems such as MALLARD profitable for both industry and public administration.
mallard_text22: MALLARD is one of the consortium’s three main projects, alongside IRYDA Plus (C-UAS) and ARES (trainer). The systems have been designed to operate within a single technological and information architecture. IRYDA provides escort, reconnaissance, and threat neutralization; MALLARD provides transport; and ARES supports training and scenario analytics. Together, they create a complete next-generation defense and logistics ecosystem.
mallard_text23: Work on the MALLARD system is entering a key phase, as the prototype of the small MALLARD-S has already been completed and is being prepared for its first test flights. In the coming weeks, ground tests will begin, followed by initial flights that will verify performance, structural stability, and autonomous takeoff and landing procedures. MBF Group will keep stakeholders informed about the stages of the program by publishing photos, recordings, and test reports. This is an important moment for the entire project – entering the flight phase confirms that Polish engineering is capable of creating world-class UAV systems. MALLARD-S is already attracting strong interest from industrial and institutional partners, and the first positive opinions regarding build quality and operational potential indicate that the project may become one of the most promising cargo UAV programs in Europe.
mallard_text24: MALLARD is one of the most advanced unmanned transport projects being developed in Europe. The system enables missions with ranges from several hundred to three thousand kilometers, with payload capacities from 300 to 1,000 kg. It can be used in civil, industrial, medical, humanitarian, and defense transport.
mallard_text25: We invite companies, institutions, and investors interested in cooperation, technology supply, cargo module development, or financial participation in the project. MBF Group S.A. is opening the possibility of capital, integration, and operational involvement. This is a unique opportunity to participate in the creation of a next-generation Polish cargo UAV system.
